title: CanonicalMediaEventV1
description: Canonical Media Event object. Governed by META v1.0. CME_FINGERPRINT_V1 defined below.
$comment:
CME_FINGERPRINT_V1 — Mint authority: META (MEDIA-EVENT-TRANSFORMER-AGENT.md)

Normalization function norm(x):
  1. trim whitespace
  2. lowercase
  3. collapse internal whitespace to single underscore
  4. remove disallowed punctuation
  5. null → empty token

Canonical concatenation (exact field order):
  CME-1.0 | actorRole_norm | action_norm | objectRole_norm | contextRole_norm |
  subtype_norm | permanence_norm | sourceReference_norm

Hash: SHA-256 → lowercase hex
ID format: CME_<sha256hex>

EXPLICIT EXCLUSIONS (do not add to fingerprint under any circumstance):
  - timestampContext (present in schema; excluded from identity by contract)
  - eventDescription (semantic drift risk)
  - eventType (const; redundant with CME-1.0 prefix)
  - fingerprintVersion (version metadata; never identity-bearing)
  - id (self-referential)

NOTE: permanence_norm always resolves to the token "permanent" (const field).
Its presence in the fingerprint is intentional — provides archival namespace
stability and mirrors the design of the CME persistence threshold declaration.

id.description: Content-derived canonical ID. Format: CME_<sha256hex>. Minted by META only.

actorRole.description: Identity-abstracted role token. No proper nouns.

eventDescription.description: Neutral declarative summary. No causal language. No proper nouns.

subtype.description: Descriptive taxonomy tag. Exactly one per CME. Rule-based assignment by META.

permanence.description: Fixed value. All CME objects are permanently archived. Participates in CME_FINGERPRINT_V1.

timestampContext.description: Season/week marker. Present in schema. EXCLUDED from CME_FINGERPRINT_V1.

fingerprintVersion.description: Nullable placeholder. Future version tagging. Does not participate in ID derivation.
